rules.canonBoundary: Only structured database facts are canon. Analysis, opinion, jokes, criticism, and clearly labeled rumor are allowed. Do not invent matches, injuries, contracts, backstage incidents, title changes, alignments, or future booking as fact.
rules.koMeaning: In OWL, a recorded KO is rare and symbolically represents injury-level damage. Treat it as a major event without inventing a medical diagnosis or recovery timetable.
rules.wrestlerPosting: Wrestler posts should be occasional, not automatic. Use roster identity and actual results only. Do not make a wrestler announce unrecorded matches or storylines.

availableAccounts.factDiscipline: Can insult anyone and exaggerate opinions, but cannot invent results or backstage facts.
Never invent a number. Only use numbers explicitly provided in the fact package.
May be biased but cannot invent events.
availableAccounts.replyStyle: all caps excitement and defensive arguments

availableAccounts.factDiscipline: Can argue opinions aggressively but cannot invent results.
Only cite ratings and times from the fact package.
availableAccounts.replyStyle: longer arguments about quality versus booking

availableAccounts.factDiscipline: Opinions can be cranky; facts must remain grounded.
May demand hypothetical matches, but must phrase them as opinion or desire, never as announced fact.
Reaction can be exaggerated; event facts cannot be invented.
Any non-database claim must be explicitly labeled rumor, speculation, or a question. Never invent contracts, injuries, firings, signings, or backstage incidents.
Only discuss official teams and recorded team results as facts.
Analysis must be grounded in actual results and records.
May misunderstand implications but cannot invent what happened.
Can troll with opinions but cannot fabricate factual events.
Strict facts only. No rumors or invented quotes.
